dependencies.relationship: internal/keys uses APIError(), NowUTC(), FormatUTC(), TokenPrefix, SetETag(), CheckETag(), CacheMiddleware(CacheNoStore) from spec 01 server_core
internal/keys queries and mutates api_keys via db.SqlDB, uses db.Executor interface, db.FormatTime(), db.WrapError(), and db.WithTx from spec 02 database_layer
All three endpoints require authentication enforced by auth_middleware spec 05; handlers read user_id and credential-type context values injected by spec 05

task_groups.title: Write Tests: Random Generation, Key Format, and Hashing

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for random string generation (alphanumeric charset, correct lengths)
details: Create internal/keys/generate_test.go
Test that GenerateAPIKey returns FullKey matching ^ak_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8}_[a-zA-Z0-9]{32}$
Test that KeyID is exactly 8 chars and secret segment is exactly 32 chars
Test that both KeyID and secret contain only [0-9A-Za-z]

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for crypto/rand failure (immediately fatal, no retries, no DB writes)
details: Use an injectable rand reader to simulate crypto/rand.Read() failures
Verify GenerateAPIKey returns non-nil error immediately on first call failure
Verify no rows are inserted into api_keys
Verify that rand failure during retry (after collision) is also immediately fatal — no further retries attempted

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for SHA-256 hashing and secret hash storage correctness
details: Verify SecretHash in APIKeyResult equals lowercase hex SHA-256 of the secret portion of FullKey
Verify SecretHash is exactly 64 chars matching ^[0-9a-f]{64}$
Verify api_keys row has secret_hash matching computed hash
Verify no column in api_keys row contains the plaintext secret

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for expiry calculation (all valid expiresDays values and invalid rejection)
details: Test expiresDays=0 → ExpiresAt==nil and expires_at IS NULL in DB
Test expiresDays=30, 60, 90 → ExpiresAt approximately now + N*24h within 2 seconds
Test invalid values (1, 15, 45, 100, -1, 365) → non-nil error returned, no DB writes
Verify expires_at in DB matches ExpiresAt in result within 1 second

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for APIKeyResult signature and public re-exports
details: Verify GenerateAPIKey accepts (db.Executor, string, int, echo.Logger) and returns (*APIKeyResult, error)
Verify all fields of APIKeyResult (FullKey, KeyID, SecretHash, ExpiresAt) are populated on success
Verify apikit.GenerateAPIKey delegates to keys.GenerateAPIKey with identical results
Verify apikit.APIKeyResult is a type alias assignable without conversion
Compile-time check: ErrKeyRevoked, ErrKeyExpired not accessible from apikit package namespace

subtasks.title: Write unit tests for type assertion transaction detection and no-package-level-logger
details: Verify source code of GenerateAPIKey contains '_, ok := tx.(*sql.DB)' type assertion pattern
Verify no wrapper type or TxDetector interface is introduced in internal/keys
Verify internal/keys has no package-level logger variable of type echo.Logger or log.Logger
Verify GenerateAPIKey function signature includes 'logger echo.Logger' parameter

task_groups.title: Write Tests: GenerateAPIKey Integration (Transaction Behavior, Revocation, Retry)

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for GenerateAPIKey with *sql.DB (internal transaction, atomicity rollback)
details: Set up SQLite in-memory DB with api_keys table schema from spec 02
Test: existing active key is revoked and new key is inserted atomically when called with *sql.DB
Test rollback: force all INSERT attempts to fail (exhaust 3 retries); verify prior key's revoked_at remains NULL
Verify no new row exists for user beyond the original after failure
Use db.WithTx or direct tx management to simulate and verify atomic behavior

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for GenerateAPIKey with *sql.Tx (caller-owned transaction, commit and rollback)
details: Test: call GenerateAPIKey with *sql.Tx from db.WithTx, commit — verify key is persisted
Test: call GenerateAPIKey with *sql.Tx, rollback — verify no key is persisted
Verify GenerateAPIKey does not call Commit or Rollback on the caller's transaction
Confirm tx is *sql.Tx detected via type assertion (not *sql.DB path)

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for GenerateAPIKey zero-rows revocation (first-time user) and expired-key revocation
details: Test first-time user (no prior keys): zero-rows-affected revocation UPDATE is silent no-op; new key inserted correctly
Test expired-but-not-revoked key: insert key with past expires_at and revoked_at IS NULL, call GenerateAPIKey, verify expired key's revoked_at is now set
Test: new key is only row with revoked_at IS NULL after expired-key scenario

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for GenerateAPIKey key_id collision retry (bounded to 3 attempts)
details: Test: pre-insert a row with the first expected key_id; verify GenerateAPIKey retries and succeeds on second attempt
Verify only one new active key exists after successful retry
Verify revocation UPDATE is NOT re-executed on retry
Test: force all 3 attempts to collide; verify GenerateAPIKey returns non-nil error after exactly 3 INSERT attempts
Verify property test TS-10-P8: total INSERT calls == K+1 for K collisions within budget, == 3 when all fail

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for GenerateAPIKey logging and non-constraint DB error handling
details: Test: inject capturing test logger; verify exactly one INFO entry emitted with user_id and key_id after successful INSERT
Test failure case: mock rand always fails; verify zero INFO log entries emitted
Test non-constraint DB error during INSERT: verify GenerateAPIKey returns error immediately, no retry (INSERT called exactly once)
Verify error from non-constraint failure surfaces as HTTP 500 'internal server error' to callers

subtasks.title: Write integration tests for one-active-key invariant and concurrent GenerateAPIKey
details: Test: user has one active key; call GenerateAPIKey; verify exactly one row with revoked_at IS NULL remains and it's the new key
Test: call GenerateAPIKey twice sequentially; verify prior key revoked, only one active key
Concurrent test: spawn 5 goroutines calling GenerateAPIKey for same user; after all complete verify exactly one row with revoked_at IS NULL
Rely on SQLite serialized writes — no application-level locking added

task_groups.title: Implement Core Package: Random Generation, Hashing, and GenerateAPIKey

subtasks.title: Implement random string generation helper (crypto/rand, alphanumeric charset, modular arithmetic)
details: Create internal/keys/generate.go
Implement private randAlphanumeric(n int) (string, error) using crypto/rand.Read()
Use 62-char alphanumeric charset [0-9A-Za-z] with byte % 62 modular arithmetic
Return error immediately on crypto/rand.Read() failure — no retries, no partial state
Ensure generated strings contain only [0-9A-Za-z] of exactly the specified length

subtasks.title: Implement APIKeyResult struct and secret hashing
details: Define APIKeyResult struct with FullKey string, KeyID string, SecretHash string, ExpiresAt *time.Time
Implement SHA-256 hashing: hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(secret)); hexHash := hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])
Construct FullKey as apikit.TokenPrefix + '_' + keyID + '_' + secret
Ensure plaintext secret is never written to any DB column

subtasks.title: Implement GenerateAPIKey: expiry validation, transaction detection, revocation UPDATE
details: Validate expiresDays ∈ {0, 30, 60, 90} — return error immediately if invalid
Detect *sql.DB vs *sql.Tx via type assertion: if _, ok := tx.(*sql.DB); ok { begin internal tx }
Execute revocation UPDATE: 'UPDATE api_keys SET revoked_at = ? WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL'
Zero rows affected is silent no-op success; proceed to INSERT
Use db.FormatTime() from spec 02 for timestamp storage

subtasks.title: Implement GenerateAPIKey: INSERT with key_id collision retry (up to 3 attempts), expiry calculation, logging
details: Generate key_id and secret via randAlphanumeric; compute secret_hash and ExpiresAt
Calculate expires_at: nil when expiresDays=0; time.Now().UTC().Add(time.Duration(expiresDays)*24*time.Hour) otherwise
INSERT into api_keys with all fields; retry up to 3 times on unique constraint violation (detected by db.WrapError())
Non-constraint-violation DB error → return error immediately, no retry
If internal transaction: rollback on any failure (revocation UPDATE is also rolled back)
On success: emit structured INFO log via logger with user_id and key_id fields; return *APIKeyResult

subtasks.title: Implement root apikit package re-exports for APIKeyResult and GenerateAPIKey
details: Add to root apikit package: type APIKeyResult = keys.APIKeyResult
Add wrapper: func GenerateAPIKey(tx db.Executor, userID string, expiresDays int, logger echo.Logger) (*APIKeyResult, error) { return keys.GenerateAPIKey(tx, userID, expiresDays, logger) }
Do NOT re-export any other symbols from internal/keys
No custom error types, constants, or helpers exposed from apikit package namespace

checks: Core generation tests pass: go test ./internal/keys/... -count=1 -run 'TestGenerate|TestFormat|TestHash|TestExpiry'
Integration tests for GenerateAPIKey pass: go test ./internal/keys/... -count=1 -run 'TestGenerateAPIKey'
Public re-export tests pass: go test ./... -count=1 -run 'TestPublicAPI'
go vet ./... reports no issues
No panic('not implemented') stubs remain in internal/keys/generate.go or root apikit re-exports

subtasks.title: Implement RegisterKeyHandlers function
details: Create internal/keys/handlers.go
Implement RegisterKeyHandlers(group *echo.Group, database *sql.DB)
Register: GET /user/keys, POST /user/keys/:key_id/refresh, DELETE /user/keys/:key_id
Do NOT apply additional cache middleware — Cache-Control: no-store is inherited from parent group's CacheMiddleware(CacheNoStore)
Store *sql.DB for use by handlers (closure or struct)

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/user/keys handler: listing query and metadata serialization
details: Read user_id from auth middleware context (spec 05)
Execute: SELECT key_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC
Serialize as JSON array; return [] (not null) when no rows — use []KeyMetadata{} initialized empty, not nil slice
Response objects contain ONLY key_id, created_at, expires_at, revoked_at — never secret_hash, secret, expires_days
Return HTTP 200 with c.JSON()

subtasks.title: Implement GET /api/v1/user/keys ETag logic (etag_ts derivation, SetETag, CheckETag, NullString handling)
details: Execute ETag query: SELECT MAX(MAX(created_at, COALESCE(revoked_at, created_at))) AS etag_ts FROM api_keys WHERE user_id = ?
Scan result as sql.NullString; if Valid==false (no keys): skip ETag setting and If-None-Match evaluation entirely
If Valid==true: reformat via FormatUTC() from spec 01, pass to SetETag(); evaluate If-None-Match via CheckETag()
If CheckETag matches: return HTTP 304 empty body
DB error on either query → HTTP 500 'internal server error' via APIError()

checks: Handler registration tests pass: go test ./internal/keys/... -count=1 -run 'TestRegisterKeyHandlers|TestListKeys|TestGetKeys'
go vet ./internal/keys/... reports no issues
Requirements 10-REQ-4.1, 10-REQ-4.2, 10-REQ-5.1–10-REQ-5.5, 10-REQ-5.E1–10-REQ-5.E3 acceptance criteria met
No panic('not implemented') stubs remain in GET handler or RegisterKeyHandlers

subtasks.title: Implement refresh handler: PAT rejection and key lookup with ownership/state validation
details: Check credential-type context value from spec 05; if PAT detected → return HTTP 401 'API key authentication required' via APIError() before any DB access
Execute: SELECT key_id, user_id, secret_hash, expires_days, expires_at, revoked_at, created_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?
If no rows → HTTP 404 'key not found'; no format validation on key_id path parameter
If row.user_id != authenticated user_id → HTTP 404 'key not found' (not 403)
If revoked_at IS NOT NULL → HTTP 400 'cannot refresh a revoked key'
If expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at < time.Now().UTC() → HTTP 400 'cannot refresh an expired key'

subtasks.title: Implement refresh handler: new secret generation, UPDATE, race condition handling, and logging
details: Generate new 32-char secret via randAlphanumeric(32); if crypto/rand fails → HTTP 500 immediately, no retry
Compute new secret_hash; recalculate expires_at from stored expires_days (nil if expires_days==0)
Execute: UPDATE api_keys SET secret_hash=?, expires_at=?, created_at=? WHERE key_id=? AND user_id=?
If rows affected == 0 → HTTP 404 'key not found' (race condition treated as missing key, not 500)
Construct FullKey = TokenPrefix + '_' + key_id + '_' + new_secret
Emit INFO log via c.Logger() with user_id and key_id after successful UPDATE; return HTTP 200 with {key, key_id, expires_at}

No panic('not implemented') stubs remain in refresh handler

subtasks.title: Implement DELETE handler: key lookup, ownership and revocation state validation
details: Execute: SELECT key_id, user_id, revoked_at FROM api_keys WHERE key_id = ?
If no rows → HTTP 404 'key not found'; no format validation on key_id path parameter
If row.user_id != authenticated user_id → HTTP 404 'key not found' (not 403)
If revoked_at IS NOT NULL → HTTP 400 'key is already revoked'
Expired-but-not-revoked keys (expires_at in past, revoked_at IS NULL) are permitted for deletion — no check on expires_at
Accept API key or PAT with keys:manage permission (enforced by auth middleware spec 05)

subtasks.title: Implement DELETE handler: revocation UPDATE, self-revocation, logging, and DB error handling
details: Execute: UPDATE api_keys SET revoked_at=? WHERE key_id=? AND user_id=? with current UTC timestamp
Self-revocation is permitted: auth middleware validated the key before handler executed — no re-validation mid-flight
Emit INFO log via c.Logger() with user_id and key_id after successful UPDATE
DB error during SELECT or UPDATE → HTTP 500 'internal server error' via APIError()
Return HTTP 200 with {key_id, revoked_at} (RFC 3339 UTC)
